summary: RE-REVIEW: telly's R4b CLOSES the BLOCK. Independent mutation (neuter isRollingMaster block) -> R4b RED (1 failed/13 passed); restore byte-identical -> 14/14 green, 0 residue. Both prior findings resolved.

findings.claim: R4 proves the rolling-recurring exemption (SPEC R4).
findings.evidence: ORIGINAL: R4 seeds a SCHEDULED rolling instance; terminalScheduleBlock short-circuits at `if (willBeScheduled) return null;` before the masterId->isRollingMaster exemption; deleting tasks.js:78-82 left all 13 tests GREEN. RESOLUTION (2026-06-26): telly added R4b (test:362-385) seeding an UNSCHEDULED rolling instance (ROLLING_UNSC_INST_ID, scheduled_at:null, rolling master ROLLING_MASTER_ID). zoe re-ran the mutation independently: neutering the `if (masterId){...isRollingMaster...}` block -> R4b turns RED (1 failed, 13 passed); the other 13 unaffected. Restore from /tmp backup -> 14/14 green; git diff --stat = 25 insertions (bert's guard only), grep ZOE-MUTATION = 0 residue. R4b is the genuine pin for the exemption branch.
findings.fix: DONE — R4b added and mutation-verified as the discriminating pin for the isRollingMaster exemption branch.

findings.claim: TEST-CATALOG.md:18/86 claim R4 covers the master_id->isRollingMaster branch.
findings.evidence: ORIGINAL: TEST-CATALOG overclaimed R4 covers the isRollingMaster branch. RESOLUTION: TEST-CATALOG.md:18 now states R4 'does NOT reach the masterId->isRollingMaster branch'; line 92 marks R4 'does NOT reach masterId->isRollingMaster branch (zoe false-green catch)'; lines 19/93 reattribute the branch to R4b ('mutation-verified RED under neutered exemption'). TEST-REVIEW.md:35 documents the correction. DB-constraint vs app-guard tension surfaced explicitly in TEST-CATALOG.md:26-29.
findings.fix: DONE — overclaim corrected; isRollingMaster branch reattributed to R4b.

findings.claim: The rolling exemption allows marking an unscheduled rolling instance terminal.
findings.evidence: For an unscheduled rolling instance the guard returns null (exempt) but the subsequent terminal write with scheduled_at=null still violates chk_task_instances_terminal_scheduled -> raw DB throw; the app-level exemption never yields a successful terminal-marking on its own. Functional correctness of the exemption is production logic (ernie's column).
findings.fix: REFER->ernie: assess whether the exemption should also set scheduled_at=NOW() or is effectively inert given the DB constraint.

refers.why: STILL OPEN: rolling exemption (tasks.js:78-82) may be functionally inert given chk_task_instances_terminal_scheduled blocks unscheduled-terminal regardless; R4b proves the exemption FIRES but the DB constraint then rejects — design question (should it set scheduled_at=NOW()?) remains ernie's column
